_analysis_notes.beat_tracker: Audacity VAMP sub-beat labels (CrescendollsAnalysis.txt), 124.114 BPM confirmed
_analysis_notes.segment_source: crescendolls_helmet.ino — SEG_A through SEG_G boundaries used verbatim, outro sub-sectioned from WAV energy analysis
structural_summary: 0–24.2s   Seg A: intro build — arpeggiated synth, energy 0.087 avg, ramps from near-silence
24.2–38.6s Seg B: locked groove — kick+bass enters, halo rotate feel, 0.120 avg (14.4s)
38.6–52.4s Seg C: escalation — additional layers stack, 0.157 avg, builds through 8 bars (13.8s)
52.4–60.0s Seg D: post-climax plateau — fullest texture in first half, 0.196 avg (7.6s)
60.0–67.4s Seg E: peak energy — highest RMS in song 0.215, snap-fills, wall-of-sound (7.4s)
67.4–84.8s Seg F: sustained peak — longest high-energy section, 0.217 avg (17.4s)
84.8–100.8s Seg G: cool-down — drops to 0.148, stripped back (16.0s)
100.8–131.5s Seg H: groove return — back to full energy 0.22+, longest groove section (30.7s)
131.5–144.0s breakdown — two brief energy drops/returns, stutter feel (12.5s)
144.0–193.5s final_groove — 50s of peak energy, highest sustained RMS in song
193.5–211.6s outro_fade — stepwise decay with gaps, ends in silence

seg_a._comment: Intro build. 0–24.2s, 12.5 bars. Arpeggiated synth only, no kick. Energy ramps slowly from near-silence (0.065) to 0.119 by end. PAL_INTRO_WARM for warm analog feel. Ears breathe, visor holds dim warm gradient. Matrix off — let the intro be clean.

cues._comment: 5.5s breath. Deep amber low, bright amber high. Restrained — this is the tease before the kick.
Very dim warm gradient — just enough to show the visor is alive.
~8s before kick enters (t≈16s), switch to a gentle beat gradient to foreshadow the rhythm. Soft decay, no sparkle yet.

seg_b._comment: Locked groove. 24.2–38.6s, 7.5 bars. Kick + bass enters. Energy 0.120 avg — solid but not peaking. PAL_MAGENTA_CYAN matches the original .ino color scheme for this segment (halo-rotate feel). Ears run beat gradient, visor gets HALO_ROTATE slow rotation.

cues._comment: Kick entry flash — marks the groove starting.
Slow halo rotation (16 beats = 4 bars per full rotation). Registry notes this was the Crescendolls Seg B pattern. Magenta halo over dark floor.
Magenta/cyan cycling beat gradient. PAL_MAGENTA_CYAN gives alternating hard magenta and cyan per beat cycle.

seg_c._comment: Escalation. 38.6–52.4s, 7.2 bars. Additional synth layers stack in. Energy climbs 0.145→0.181 through the 8 bars. PAL_DISCOVERY replaces PAL_MAGENTA_CYAN — widening the color vocabulary as the song opens up. Beat gradient more aggressive, visor switches to beat wipe for directional motion.

cues._comment: Palette-change punch on the seg C downbeat.
Bottom-to-top wipe each beat. The directionality mirrors the 'stacking' feel of the music as layers are added.
Full Discovery palette cycling. Escalation is visible in the higher decay and sparkle vs seg_b.

seg_d._comment: Post-climax plateau. 52.4–60.0s, 4 bars. Fullest texture in first half, 0.196 avg. The .ino's Seg D had alternating violet column fills and gold gravity pulses. PAL_DISCOVERY. Ears stay on beat gradient, visor gets a beat wipe in the opposite direction (top-to-bottom) to signal the descent/plateau feel.

cues._comment: Crescendolls-example params from registry: deep indigo base, strong downbeat flash, hi-hat sparkle.
Top-to-bottom wipe — contrasts with seg_c's upward motion. 'Gravity after the upward fill' as the .ino described it.

seg_e._comment: Peak energy. 60.0–67.4s, 3.8 bars. Highest RMS in first half (0.215). Everything is at maximum. Switch visor to HALO_ROTATE fast to convey the spinning, wall-of-sound energy. Ears at max beat gradient aggression.

cues._comment: Peak energy arrival flash — brightest single moment in the first half.
Fast halo rotation (8 beats = 2 bars). Faster than seg_b's 16-beat rotation — the energy increase is felt in the speed.

seg_f._comment: Sustained peak. 67.4–84.8s, 9 bars. Longest high-energy section, 0.217 avg. Holds everything at max. Visor switches to BEAT_WIPE inner-to-outer for a new directional feel after seg_e's rotation. Keep ears at near-max but fractionally lighter than seg_e so final_groove has room to escalate further.

cues._comment: Inner-to-outer wipe — new direction for visual variety in the long sustained section.

seg_g._comment: Cool-down. 84.8–100.8s, 8.3 bars. Drops to 0.148 avg RMS — stripped back, lighter percussion. PAL_COOL_SWEEP provides a cooler color contrast. Beat gradient softened, visor back to halo rotate (slow again) to settle the energy.

cues._comment: Cyan-to-violet cycling. Softer than seg_f — deliberate step-down before the big groove return.
Back to slow 16-beat rotation. Cool cyan halo over dark floor — spacious, breathing.
~4s before seg_h groove return (t≈96.8s), beat gradient builds tension. Warm gold reappears after the cool-sweep section. (Thomas: EKG replaced — diffuser makes individual LED pulses invisible.)
Transition visor to PAL_DISCOVERY warm gradient ahead of the groove return.

seg_h._comment: Groove return. 100.8–131.5s, 15.9 bars. Energy jumps back to 0.22+. The biggest sustained groove section — longer than seg_f. Back to full PAL_DISCOVERY, maximum beat gradient. Visor gets outer-to-inner wipe (new direction, contrasts with seg_f's inner-to-outer) to mark the return as distinct.

cues._comment: Groove return flash — includes matrix. The biggest re-entry punch so far.
Outer-to-inner wipe — never used yet, feels like a 'gathering' motion matching the groove re-consolidating.

breakdown._comment: Two stutter drops. 131.5–144.0s. Energy stutters: 0.22 → 0.07 (t≈133s) → brief return (t≈138s) → drop again (t≈141s) → recovery (t≈143.5s). These feel like DJ-style filter kills. PAL_NEON for a lurid, disorienting color shift during the silence. Ears breathe during the drops.

cues._comment: Fast ~1.8s breath during the drop. PAL_NEON's hot pink/lime is jarring — intentionally wrong-feeling.
Dim neon gradient — disorients vs the sustained warm Discovery energy.
~6s in: second drop recovery (t≈138s in song). Beat gradient rebuilds — signals the final groove is coming. (Thomas: EKG replaced — diffuser makes individual LED pulses invisible.)

final_groove._comment: Final long groove. 144.0–193.5s, 25.6 bars. Longest single sustained section in the song. Peak RMS sustained for 50 continuous seconds. Maximum everything — this is the climax. Beat gradient at absolute max params. Visor cycles through all three wipe directions across the section in 3 phases (~16 bars each).

cues._comment: Biggest flash in the song. Longest decay (18 × 10ms = 180ms settling), includes matrix.
Phase 1 (0–16 bars ≈ 31s): bottom-to-top wipe. Revisiting the seg_c direction at maximum energy.
Absolute maximum. decay_steepness=200, downbeat_flash=240, sparkle=100. Registry Crescendolls example values pushed to ceiling.
Phase 2 (bars 16–25 ≈ 31s–49.5s): inner-to-outer wipe. Color shifts to palette index 4 for variety.

outro_fade._comment: Stepwise decay. 193.5–211.6s, ~9.4 bars. Energy falls through stutter gaps (0.22→0.07→0.09→0.16→0.08→0.09→0.07→0.01→0). The song dissolves. Three acts: (1) 0–3s rapid drop, (2) 3–14s stutter gaps with breathe, (3) 14–18.1s full silence/fade. PAL_INTRO_WARM bookends the intro's warm amber.

cues._comment: Fast ~2.2s breath as the energy drops. Warm amber mirrors the intro's palette.
Dim warm gradient. The song dissolves back to the same visual state it started in.
Final 4s of silence: very slow ambient breathe, nearly off. Song ends.
Nearly off visor — just a ghost of the warm gradient as the track goes silent.
